unicorns.name: Flipkart
unicorns.problem_statement: Online shopping infrastructure didn't exist in India; limited catalog, trust issues
unicorns.solution: Full-stack marketplace with cash-on-delivery, logistics, payments

unicorns.name: Paytm
unicorns.problem_statement: Digital payments didn't exist in India; mobile payment adoption was zero
unicorns.solution: Mobile wallet and payment gateway for merchants and consumers
key_success_factors: Expansion to multiple use cases (travel, utilities, etc.)

unicorns.name: OYO Rooms
unicorns.problem_statement: Budget hotel chains didn't exist; fragmented unorganized hotel sector
unicorns.solution: Standardized budget hotel chain through partnership model
unicorns.initial_target: Budget travelers, tier 2-3 cities, business travelers

unicorns.name: BYJU'S
unicorns.problem_statement: Rote learning in Indian education system; limited access to quality tutoring
unicorns.solution: AI-powered adaptive learning platform for K-12 students
unicorns.initial_target: Urban K-12 students, initially through after-school tuitions

unicorns.name: Unacademy
unicorns.problem_statement: Quality competitive exam coaching (IIT-JEE, UPSC) was expensive and limited to metros
unicorns.solution: Live and recorded classes from top educators on YouTube/app
unicorns.initial_target: Engineering and civil service aspirants, tier 2-3 cities
key_success_factors: Focus on high-value use cases (IIT-JEE, UPSC)

unicorns.name: CRED
unicorns.problem_statement: Credit card bill payment had high friction; rewards weren't accessible
unicorns.solution: Gamified credit card bill payment app with instant rewards
unicorns.initial_target: High-income credit card users, metro cities
key_success_factors: Solved real friction point for affluent users
unicorns.notable: Fastest to unicorn among recent Indian startups

unicorns.name: Nykaa
unicorns.problem_statement: Beauty products in India were fragmented; no trusted online marketplace
unicorns.solution: Centralized online marketplace for cosmetics and skincare
unicorns.initial_target: Urban females, 18-35, middle to upper-middle class
key_success_factors: First woman founder to lead IPO in India

unicorns.name: Razorpay
unicorns.problem_statement: Payment gateways for Indian businesses were expensive, complex, and limited
unicorns.solution: Easy-to-integrate payments platform for online and offline transactions
key_success_factors: Expanded to multiple products (lending, payroll, etc.)
